testCases.query: Write a Playwright E2E test for the checkout flow
expectedBehavior: Uses semantic locators: getByRole, getByLabel, getByPlaceholder over CSS selectors
Uses Playwright auto-wait instead of hardcoded waitForTimeout calls
Follows locator priority: getByRole > getByLabel > getByPlaceholder > getByTestId
Structures test with clear user flow steps

testCases.query: Set up page objects for my Playwright test suite
expectedBehavior: Creates page object classes with locators defined in the constructor
Includes action methods for user interactions and assertion methods for verification
Encapsulates page-specific logic to avoid duplication across tests
Supports visual regression with toHaveScreenshot in page assertions

testCases.query: Add accessibility checks to my Playwright E2E tests
expectedBehavior: Uses @axe-core/playwright AxeBuilder for full-page WCAG scanning
Targets WCAG 2.2 AA compliance with wcag2a, wcag2aa, wcag22aa tags
Tests interactive states (modals, dropdowns) for accessibility violations
Asserts results.violations is empty for each page

testCases.query: How should I test GitHub API interactions in my E2E tests without hitting real APIs?
expectedBehavior: Recommends emulate backends as FIRST CHOICE for deterministic E2E
Provides seed config pattern for reproducible test data
Shows per-worker port isolation for parallel Playwright execution
Explains benefits over MSW/Nock: full state machines, CI-friendly

testCases.query: Set up AI-powered test generation with Playwright 1.58
expectedBehavior: Describes the Planner/Generator/Healer three-agent workflow
References npx playwright init-agents --loop=claude for Claude Code
Mentions token-efficient CLI mode for reduced context overhead
Explains Healer auto-fixes: selectors, waits, dynamic content

testCases.query: Write unit tests for my pure utility function that calculates discounts
expectedBehavior: Should NOT recommend Playwright or E2E patterns for pure logic
Should redirect to testing-unit skill for isolated business logic tests
Does not suggest page objects or browser testing for utility functions
